L1.area: HTTP request processing and access rule matching

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where an attacker can craft HTTP requests containing relative path components that bypass access control rules. The raw, un-normalized request path is used during rule evaluation, allowing requests to match permissive rules while the upstream server normalizes the path and serves protected resources.

L3.area: HTTP request processing and access rule matching
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where an attacker can craft HTTP requests containing relative path components that bypass access control rules. The raw, un-normalized request path is used during rule evaluation, allowing requests to match permissive rules while the upstream server normalizes the path and serves protected resources.

ground_truth.reason: The request URL path is not normalized before rule matching, allowing path traversal sequences to bypass access control rules and reach protected upstream resources.
